'''Aluminite''' was a commercially valuable [[Ore/Legends|ore]]. The [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] of the [[Kadal Asteroid Field]] in the [[Bestal system/Legends|Bestal system]]<ref name="Polyhedron" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Varada sector/Legends|Varada sector]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> possessed rich aluminite deposits.<ref name="Polyhedron" />

During the existence of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the company [[Fax Ventures]] engaged in the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] of aluminite in the Kadal Asteroid Field followed by the processing of the ore on the nearby [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bestal Three]]. While those operations were highly profitable for the company, they nevertheless only served as cover for an even more lucrative venture—the harvesting of the [[Seed/Legends|seeds]] of Bestal Three's [[vortal tree]]s for medicinal purposes.<ref name="Polyhedron" />

==Behind the scenes==
Aluminite was introduced in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' franchise in "[[Adversaries: Characters for the Star Wars: New Republic Campaign]]," a [[roleplaying game]] source article authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[Polyhedron 97|ninety-seventh issue]] of the ''[[Polyhedron]]'' magazine in [[July]] [[1994]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Polyhedron" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> In the real world, [[Wikipedia:Aluminite|aluminite]] is a white, yellow, or gray [[Wikipedia:Mineral|mineral]] that principally contains [[Wikipedia:Aluminium|aluminium]].<ref name="Mindat">{{WebCite|url=https://www.mindat.org/min-154.html|text=Aluminite: Mineral information, data and localities.|date=2025-01-07|work=[[Wikipedia:Mindat.org|Mindat.org]]|archivedate=20241223123624}}</ref>
